rules.name: reject_short_random_alphanumeric
rules.description: Reject product if it is a short random alphanumeric token (e.g., sow0e7ym, iobqd8a9, ay4dy8o5)

rules.name: reject_month_day_phrase
rules.description: Reject product if it is a month+day phrase (e.g., May 4th, June 4th)

rules.name: reject_pure_number_product
rules.description: Reject product if it is a pure number or numeric phrase (e.g., around 1897, of 12M, a 24, up 130)

rules.name: reject_date_fragment_product
rules.description: Reject product if it contains date-like fragments

rules.name: reject_weak_preposition_start_product
rules.description: Reject product if it starts with weak preposition/determiner unless in known product list

rules.name: reject_verb_phrase_product
rules.description: Reject product if it starts with a verb (looks like a sentence fragment, not a product name)

rules.name: reject_long_phrase_product
rules.description: Reject product if longer than 6 words and not a known valid product
examples: data then skip the interface entirely Agents
everything when you build products for Finance

rules.name: reject_sentence_fragment_product
rules.description: Reject product if contains sentence punctuation in a multi-word phrase
examples: CI failures. Set up always-on agents
just built issue was the initial characters

rules.name: reject_generic_for_every_product
rules.description: Reject product containing 'for every/your/all' pattern — these are descriptive phrases not products
examples: Git semantics for every file your agent

rules.name: reject_url_fragment_product
rules.description: Reject product if it contains URL fragments

actor_rules.name: former_employer_is_not_actor
actor_rules.description: Former employer mentioned in context (e.g., '前 Meta FAIR Director') is not the actor. The actor is the person or new company.
examples: Meta mentioned as former employer, actor should be 田渊栋 or Recursive

actor_rules.name: product_is_not_organization_actor
actor_rules.description: Product/model name (e.g., GPT-5.5, Gemini) should not be used as actor unless the source is the official product account.
examples: GPT-5.5 as actor → should be OpenAI
Gemini as actor → should be Google

actor_rules.name: source_account_as_actor_only_if_official
actor_rules.description: Social media account name can be actor only if it's the official source making an announcement about itself.

actor_rules.name: prefer_empty_actor_over_wrong_actor
actor_rules.description: Leave actor empty rather than populating it with a wrong organization/product name.
examples: arxiv as actor for pricing → wrong, should be empty
